primitiveTransportNoEndpointStatus.label: primitive transport has no endpoint boundary term
primitiveTransportNoEndpointStatus.reason: For compact smooth half-line tests, F(0)=G(0)=0.  Since the parity kernels decay as either variable tends to infinity, integration by parts in the identity H=P_xy gives no lower or upper endpoint contribution.  Hence the coordinate form equals the mixed Volterra form exactly.

canonicalPrimitiveBoundaryZeroStatus.label: canonical primitive boundary form is zero
canonicalPrimitiveBoundaryZeroStatus.reason: With Q_Phi defined as the mixed/Volterra transport of the original coordinate form, beta_bdy=Q_original-Q_Phi is identically zero.  Thus D_bdy is the zero operator on the completed trace range.

zeroBoundaryDescendsStatus.label: zero boundary form descends through R_global
zeroBoundaryDescendsStatus.reason: The zero form annihilates ker R_global and is bounded in every transported trace norm, so it descends trivially with D_bdy=0.

comparisonReducesToDqZeroStatus.label: D_bdy >= D_q reduces to D_q=0
comparisonReducesToDqZeroStatus.reason: Because D_bdy=0, the sufficient comparison D_bdy>=D_q is equivalent to D_q=0 on the relevant completed primitive trace range.  A positive hidden boundary repair cannot be supplied by the primitive Green identity.

dqZeroOnPrimitiveImageStatus.label: minimal quotient repair vanishes on the primitive trace image
dqZeroOnPrimitiveImageStatus.reason: This has not been proved.  It is now the exact replacement for the failed boundary-repair route.  One must either prove D_q R F=0 for every primitive image F, or prove Q_Phi>=0 directly on that primitive image.
dqZeroOnPrimitiveImageStatus.blocker: Characterize the primitive trace image Y=R({F: F'=original test}) inside X_R and prove D_q|_Y=0, or prove the equivalent direct positivity of Q_Phi on the primitive image.

originalPositivityFromPrimitiveTransport.label: original positivity from primitive transport
originalPositivityFromPrimitiveTransport.reason: The primitive transport is exact, but the quotient Schur theorem only gives automatic positivity on ker R_global.  Since primitive images are not contained in ker R_global and D_bdy=0, original positivity still requires D_q to vanish on the primitive trace image or a direct primitive-image positivity theorem.
originalPositivityFromPrimitiveTransport.blocker: Characterize the primitive trace image Y=R({F: F'=original test}) inside X_R and prove D_q|_Y=0, or prove the equivalent direct positivity of Q_Phi on the primitive image.

integrationByPartsProof: Start from P(x,y)=int_x^inf int_y^inf H(u,v)dudv, so H=P_xy.
Let F(u)=int_0^u f(x)dx and G(v)=int_0^v g(y)dy.
Then int int HFG = [P_yFG]_{u=0}^{inf}-int int P_y f G.
The boundary term vanishes because F(0)=0 and P_y(inf,v)=0.
A second integration by parts gives int int P f g; the v-boundary vanishes because G(0)=0 and P(x,inf)=0.

operators.Dbdy: 0 on X_R for the primitive transport already identified with Q_Phi
operators.Dq: minimal quotient/Douglas repair from the closed Schur theorem

correctedNextProofTarget: Characterize the primitive trace image Y=R({F: F'=original test}) inside X_R and prove D_q|_Y=0, or prove the equivalent direct positivity of Q_Phi on the primitive image.
